title: coronilla minima subsp. lotoides
intro: Coronilla minima subsp. lotoides, also known as small crown vetch or lotoides crown vetch, is a sub-species of the Coronilla minima plant, belonging to the Fabaceae family of legumes. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Coronilla minima subsp. lotoides include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is used in the form of a tincture or dried herb for its medicinal and herbal preparations.
